title: JSON to TypeScript Interface
context: Converting raw JSON API responses into robust TypeScript interfaces.
task: Analyze the provided JSON payload and generate the corresponding TypeScript interfaces or types.
reasoning: Traverse the JSON tree. Infer primitive types (string, number, boolean). Detect arrays and nested objects. Handle potentially nullable fields if arrays contain mixed types.
examples: Input: `{"user": {"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}}`. Output: `interface User { id: number; name: string; }`
constraints: Do not output anything other than valid TypeScript code.
rules: 1. Create separate interfaces for nested objects. 2. Use PascalCase for interface names. 3. Mark fields as optional (?) if they seem sparse, though default to required.
criteria: Code must compile under strict TypeScript settings.
errorPolicy: If JSON is completely invalid, return an error message instead of code.
